summary: dotenvv-tool postinstall harvests host/user info and filesystem data
details: Package name closely resembles the popular `dotenv` package (likely typosquat). The shipped `postinstall.js` lifecycle script combines `os` (reading `os.hostname()`, `os.userInfo()`, `os.homedir()`), filesystem probing via `fs.existsSync(...)` against multiple paths, and outbound `http` networking — the canonical install-time host-recon plus exfiltration shape. This causes any developer or CI system running `npm install` to leak host identifiers and filesystem-presence information to the package author over the network without consent. The combination of typosquat-style naming, automatic execution at install via postinstall, host/user enumeration, filesystem probing, and outbound HTTP is unambiguous installer-side harm.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
